name: Ioflupane I-123
description: Ioflupane (I-123) is a radiopharmaceutical used to image dopamine neurons and diagnose Parkinsonian syndromes.
indication: Ioflupane I-123 is a SPECT (single photon emission computerized tomography) agent used to distinguish between Parkinson’s syndrome tremors and essential tremor.
mechanismOfAction: Iodine-123 labeled ioflupane binds to presynaptic dopamine transporters. When Iodine-123 decays, a gammay ray is emmitted and detected through SPECT.
pharmacodynamics: iodine-123 labeled ioflupanebinds selectively to striatal presynaptic dopamine neurons by binding reversibly to presynaptic dopamine transporters.

interactions.description: Pseudoephedrine may decrease effectiveness of Ioflupane I-123 as a diagnostic agent.
